title: Dense Greek Fish Grill
description: A hearty, comforting dish featuring mustardy flavors and anti-inflammatory-friendly ingredients, perfect for a lazy evening on the grill.
instructions: Preheat your robata grill to medium-high heat, or set up your grill for indirect grilling.

In a large bowl, whisk together the Greek yogurt, Dijon mustard, lemon juice, garlic, and a pinch of salt and pepper. Add the fish fillets and marinate for at least 30 minutes, or up to 2 hours in the refrigerator.
Remove the fish from the marinade, letting any excess liquid drip off. Brush the fish with olive oil on both sides.
Grill the fish for 8-10 minutes per side, or until it reaches an internal temperature of 145°F. Transfer the fish to a plate and let it rest for 5 minutes before serving.
In a small bowl, mix together the dasi broth and chopped fresh dill. Brush the mixture onto the grilled fish.
Serve the fish hot, garnished with Kalamata olives and a side of your favorite grains or roasted vegetables.
Pour the remaining daish broth mixture over the fish and garnish with lemon wedges, if desired.
